git-foundations.title: Git Foundations
git-foundations.description: Build one local project history step by step, reapply the same Git habits in one continuing module project, and prove them independently in a fresh capstone.
git-foundations.moreComingSoon: Branches, merges, remotes, and GitHub collaboration belong to later Git courses.

git-foundations-module-1-start-tracking.title: Start the Community Site Repository
git-foundations-module-1-start-tracking.description: Initialize one guided repository, read its state, and record its first snapshot.
outcomes: Explain why one project with history is safer than duplicate “final” folders.
Initialize community-site on main and configure the supplied local identity.
Carry the same project files from untracked state into a staged proposal.
Record and verify the first clean commit.
why: Gives every later Git command a concrete repository to work with.
Builds the inspect, act, verify rhythm used throughout the course.

git-foundations-module-2-meaningful-hist.title: Build Meaningful History
git-foundations-module-2-meaningful-hist.description: Continue the same repository with exact edits, focused staging, and history inspection.
outcomes: Use git diff to review unstaged and staged content.
Choose related files for one focused snapshot.
Create concise commits while leaving unrelated work outside them.
Read history with git log --oneline and inspect a commit with git show.
why: Turns raw edits into focused snapshots another developer can review.
Makes commit history useful as documentation, not just storage.

git-foundations-module-3-manage-tracked-.title: Maintain a Clean Repository
git-foundations-module-3-manage-tracked-.description: Continue the same repository through tracked path changes, ignore rules, and safe recovery.
outcomes: Rename and remove tracked files with Git-aware commands.
Keep generated and local-only files outside future snapshots.
Stop tracking a file while preserving its working copy.
Correct staged and unstaged mistakes without rewriting commits.
why: Keeps the repository accurate as files change names, disappear, or become generated output.
Builds safe correction habits without rewriting existing history.

git-foundations-module-4-final-capstone.title: Final Capstone
git-foundations-module-4-final-capstone.description: Build a fresh neighborhood-guide repository through six cumulative steps.
outcomes: Create a clean repository from an ordinary project folder.
Build four purposeful commits whose boundaries match the requested work.
Keep generated output ignored and obsolete files out of HEAD.
Verify the final history and clean working state before handoff.
why: Combines initialization, focused commits, file management, and verification in one realistic handoff.
Produces a complete local history that explains the work from beginning to end.

git-foundations-git-foundations-section-.title: From Ordinary Folder to First Snapshot
git-foundations-git-foundations-section-.description: Carry community-site from an ordinary folder through initialization, staging, its first commit, and clean verification.
bullets: Record the First Community Site Snapshot

git-foundations-git-foundations-section-.title: Parallel Module Project
git-foundations-git-foundations-section-.description: Start the cumulative volunteer-hub repository and record its first clean snapshot.
bullets: Module Project: Start Volunteer Hub History

git-foundations-git-foundations-section-.title: From Exact Edits to Focused History
git-foundations-git-foundations-section-.description: Continue community-site with two exact file edits, focused staging, separate commits, and history inspection.
bullets: Stage One Purpose at a Time

git-foundations-git-foundations-section-.title: Parallel Module Project
git-foundations-git-foundations-section-.description: Continue the same volunteer-hub history and record the exact heading and spacing changes separately.
bullets: Module Project: Build Volunteer Hub History

git-foundations-git-foundations-section-.title: From Tracked Changes to Clean Handoff
git-foundations-git-foundations-section-.description: Continue community-site through tracked path maintenance, ignore rules, and safe layer-specific recovery.
bullets: Keep Generated Files Out of History

git-foundations-git-foundations-section-.title: Parallel Module Project
git-foundations-git-foundations-section-.description: Finish the cumulative volunteer-hub repository with a clean maintenance snapshot.
bullets: Module Project: Clean the Volunteer Hub Repository

git-foundations-git-foundations-section-.title: Final Capstone
git-foundations-git-foundations-section-.description: Build one fresh neighborhood-guide repository through six cumulative steps.
